page: Microsoft Azure E48as_v5 is an EASv5-series virtual machine built on the x86_64 architecture, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor. This configuration delivers 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores with multithreading enabled at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The processor features 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, supported by virtualization technologies such as AMD SVM. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core.

This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external Premium Storage options. It also does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The hypervisor layer is managed by Microsoft Hyper-V.

Benchmark data indicates strong CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core metrics placing in the top 25%. Passmark CPU and memory scores are also in the top 25%, alongside strong performance in image processing, encryption, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks remains in the middle 50%, as do Geekbench multi-core and single-core scores. For language model workloads, the server achieves strong text generation speeds with small 135M models, but drops to the bottom 10% for large 70B models, highlighting a performance tradeoff for large-scale AI inference.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E48as_v5 offers a high-density memory profile relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. It is suited for relational databases, enterprise applications, memory-cached web serving, and moderate data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E48as_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. It operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and lacks local storage, relying instead on external Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks show strong CPU and memory capabilities, with stress-ng and Passmark CPU scores ranking in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average tier. While the instance handles small-model LLM text generation efficiently, performance drops significantly for larger models. This server is suited for memory-intensive workloads, including databases, enterprise applications, and caching layers.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48as_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB of RAM, and a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor, suited for databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E48as_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and a dedicated AMD EPYC processor, designed for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and high memory density for database and enterprise workloads.

Strong CPU and memory performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
No local storage, requiring external Premium Storage configurations
High memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core
Suited for relational databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications
